tool_name: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation

inputs.name: Sound Power Level of Source (L_W)
inputs.note: From equipment data sheet or measured per ISO 3744/3746

inputs.name: Excess Attenuation (barriers, air absorption, ground) (A_exc)
inputs.note: Per ISO 9613-2: sum of A_atm + A_gr + A_bar; use 3 dB as conservative indoor default

options.label: 85 dB(A) — NIOSH REL / EU Action Level
80 dB(A) — EU Lower Exposure Action Value

formulas: A_div_db = 20 * Math.log10(distance_m) + 10 * Math.log10(4 * Math.PI / directivity_factor_Q)   // [dB] Geometric Spreading Attenuation (A_div) Reference: ISO 9613-2:1996 §7.1 — geometrical divergence
DeltaL_sources_db = 10 * Math.log10(num_identical_sources)   // [dB] Multi-Source Combination Correction Reference: Energy summation: 10 identical sources → +10 dB

Lp_receiver_db = Lw_source_db + DeltaL_sources_db - A_div_db - excess_attenuation_db   // [dB(A)] Receiver Sound Pressure Level (L_p) Reference: ISO 9613-2 §4 — basic outdoor sound propagation equation

T_perm_h = (osha_criterion_db === 90) ? 8 / Math.pow(2, (Lp_receiver_dba - 90)/5) : 8 / Math.pow(2, (Lp_receiver_dba - 85)/3)   // [h] OSHA Permissible Exposure Duration at L_p (T_perm) Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95 Table G-16 (5 dB exchange rate) / NIOSH 3 dB exchange rate
dose_pct = (exposure_hours_per_shift / T_perm_h) * 100   // [%] Noise Dose (%) Reference: OSHA dose D = Σ(C_i/T_i) × 100; dose ≤ 100% mandatory

dB_reduction_needed = Math.max(0, Lp_receiver_dba - osha_criterion_db)   // [dB] Required Attenuation for Compliance Reference: Hierarchy of noise control: engineering controls first per ISO 1996-1 §8

V1.error_msg: Very close range to high-power source: near-field effects dominate — ISO 9613 far-field model not valid below 1 m

V2.error_msg: Noise dose > 100%: OSHA PEL exceeded — mandatory engineering controls, hearing protection, or exposure reduction

V3.error_msg: SPL > 115 dB(A): OSHA ceiling limit — no unprotected exposure permissible regardless of duration

smart_warnings.message: Noise dose > 50%: OSHA Hearing Conservation Program mandatory — audiometric testing, training, hearing protection per 29 CFR 1910.95(c)
SPL >10 dB over criterion: hearing protection alone insufficient — engineering noise control required per ISO 1996-1 hierarchy
Required attenuation > 15 dB: barrier or enclosure study needed — verify with ISO 9613-2 barrier insertion loss calculation
Multiple sources (>10): total SPL increase plateaus — adding sources beyond 10× gives <1 dB additional increment
Permissible exposure < 1 hour: extreme noise environment — restrict access, mandatory enclosures, and continuous monitoring
Low excess attenuation at long range: re-evaluate air absorption per ISO 9613-1 — significant at >100 m above 1 kHz
